rationale: Wrap the proof in `if H { ... }` so the antecedent (both `next_ensures` instantiations) becomes a body-level hypothesis (the previous attempt did this but stopped at re-asserting individual conjuncts). Inside, materialise every conjunct of `next_ensures` for r1 and r2 separately, then case-split on `(r1.0, r2.0)`: in the (ok, ok) case derive constants-preservation from `next`, end_point-equality from the revealed `invariants` (`constants.me == netc.my_end_point()`), and history-equality from the shared `pre_netc.history() + er.ios` skeleton; in the (false, false) case the only deducible fact is `r1.0 == r2.0`; in the mixed case `r1.0 != r2.0` shows det_equal's first conjunct already collapses. Finally re-anchor `event_seq == recvs + clocks + sends` on both runs so z3 has the EventResultsView field-by-field skeleton.
